overview: Startup India Initiative is a flagship Government of India program launched in 2016 by DPIIT to foster innovation, support entrepreneurs, and drive sustainable economic growth. It provides recognition, tax benefits, easier compliance, IPR fast-tracking, and access to funding schemes like Fund of Funds and Seed Fund Scheme. The initiative aims to build a robust startup ecosystem across India through simplification, handholding, funding support, and industry-academia partnerships.
objectives: Reduce regulatory burden on startups to allow focus on core business and lower compliance costs
Provide funding support through Fund of Funds with a corpus of INR 10,000 crore
Offer tax exemptions on profits for 3 years under Section 80-IAC
Enable fast-tracking of patent applications with 80% rebate in fees
Facilitate easier public procurement by exempting startups from prior experience/turnover criteria
Support faster exit for startups within 90 days under Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code
Promote industry-academia partnerships and incubation through initiatives like Atal Innovation Mission
Encourage innovation-driven manufacturing and development of long gestation technologies
eligibility: An entity must be incorporated or registered in India as a private limited company, partnership firm, or limited liability partnership; be within 10 years of incorporation/registration (up to 20 years for DeepTech startups); have annual turnover not exceeding INR 100 crore (INR 300 crore for DeepTech); work towards innovation, development, or improvement of products/services or have a scalable business model with high potential for employment generation or wealth creation; and not be formed by splitting up or reconstructing an existing business.
benefits: DPIIT recognition enables access to tax benefits including 100% tax exemption on profits for 3 consecutive years under Section 80-IAC, exemption under Section 56(2)(VIIB) for investments above fair market value up to INR 25 crore, self-certification under 9 labour and 3 environment laws, fast-tracking of patent applications with 80% rebate in fees, exemption from Earnest Money Deposit in government tenders, relaxed norms in public procurement, faster winding up within 90 days, access to Fund of Funds and Seed Fund Scheme, credit guarantee support, and participation in initiatives like MAARG mentorship platform, BHASKAR knowledge registry, and National Startup Awards.
financial_support: Financial support is provided through the Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) with a total corpus of INR 10,000 crore, managed by SIDBI, which invests in SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that in turn fund startups. The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) provides financial assistance for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialisation. The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) offers credit guarantee up to ₹20 crore per borrower for loans from Member Institutions. Additionally, the Fund of Funds 2.0 supports deep tech, early-stage, manufacturing, and sector/stage agnostic startups through segmented AIFs.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 20 Lakhs from SISFS for early-stage validation
application_process: 1. Register on the Startup India portal (startupindia.gov.in). 2. Apply for DPIIT recognition via the National Single Window System (NSWS) at nsws.gov.in by adding 'Registration as a Startup' to approvals. 3. Fill the application form with entity details, innovation description, and scalability. 4. Upload required documents: Certificate of Incorporation/Registration, PAN, Memorandum of Association/LLP Deed, Board Resolution (if any), annual accounts and income tax returns for last three financial years. 5. Submit the application and await approval (typically 2-7 business days). 6. Upon recognition, access benefits via Startup India portal and apply for specific schemes like Seed Fund Scheme or tax exemptions through respective portals.
required_documents: Memorandum of Association for Pvt. Ltd. / LLP Deed
Annual Accounts of the startup for the last three financial years
Income Tax returns for the last three financial years
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry
caveats: Tax benefits under Section 80-IAC require separate Inter-Ministerial Board (IMB) approval
Startup status expires when turnover exceeds INR 100 Cr (INR 300 Cr for DeepTech) or after 10 years (20 years for DeepTech)
Each incentive or exemption is governed by its own rules and requires separate application and qualification
DPIIT recognition only makes startups eligible to apply for benefits; it does not automatically entitle them to all listed benefits
